갈라디아서 4:1.content: But I say that so long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a bondservant, though he is lord of all,

갈라디아서 4:2.content: but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed by the father.

갈라디아서 4:3.content: So we also, when we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world.

갈라디아서 4:4.content: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,

갈라디아서 4:5.content: that he might redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of children.

갈라디아서 4:6.content: And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, “Abba, Father!”

갈라디아서 4:7.content: So you are no longer a bondservant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

갈라디아서 4:8.content: However at that time, not knowing God, you were in bondage to those who by nature are not gods.

갈라디아서 4:9.content: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, why do you turn back again to the weak and miserable elemental principles, to which you desire to be in bondage all over again?

갈라디아서 4:10.content: You observe days, months, seasons, and years.

갈라디아서 4:11.content: I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you.

갈라디아서 4:12.content: I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,

갈라디아서 4:13.content: but you know that because of weakness in the flesh I preached the Good News to you the first time.

갈라디아서 4:14.content: That which was a temptation to you in my flesh, you didn’t despise nor reject; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

갈라디아서 4:15.content: What was the blessing you enjoyed? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me.

갈라디아서 4:16.content: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?

갈라디아서 4:17.content: They zealously seek you in no good way. No, they desire to alienate you, that you may seek them.

갈라디아서 4:18.content: But it is always good to be zealous in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you.

갈라디아서 4:19.content: My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you—

갈라디아서 4:20.content: but I could wish to be present with you now, and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.

갈라디아서 4:21.content: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law?

갈라디아서 4:22.content: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the servant, and one by the free woman.

갈라디아서 4:23.content: However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise.

갈라디아서 4:24.content: These things contain an allegory, for these are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children to bondage, which is Hagar.

갈라디아서 4:25.content: For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and answers to the Jerusalem that exists now, for she is in bondage with her children.

갈라디아서 4:26.content: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

갈라디아서 4:27.content: For it is written, “Rejoice, you barren who don’t bear. Break out and shout, you who don’t travail. For the desolate have more children than her who has a husband.”

갈라디아서 4:28.content: Now we, brothers, as Isaac was, are children of promise.

갈라디아서 4:29.content: But as then, he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.

갈라디아서 4:30.content: However what does the Scripture say? “Throw out the servant and her son, for the son of the servant will not inherit with the son of the free woman.”

갈라디아서 4:31.content: So then, brothers, we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman.
